pattern: A monotonic stack computes, for each element, how many subarrays it is the minimum of; multiplying element by that count and summing gives the answer in linear time.
hints: For each element arr[i], count how many subarrays have arr[i] as their minimum — then add arr[i] * count to the answer instead of enumerating every subarray.

Use a monotonic stack to efficiently find, for each index i, the nearest smaller element to the left (left boundary) and to the right (right boundary); the count of subarrays where arr[i] is the minimum is the product of those two distances.

Push indices onto an ascending stack; when arr[i] is smaller than the top, pop and compute that element's left/right span using the previous stack element and i; use strict comparison on one side to handle duplicates without double-counting.

edge_inputs.description: all equal elements — every element is every subarray's minimum
strictly decreasing — leftmost element is minimum only of first subarray, rightmost is minimum of all
strictly increasing — each element is minimum only of subarrays it starts
adjacent duplicates at minimum — double-counting risk

stress.description: large array with repeated values to stress duplicate handling
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    return [[rng.randrange(1, 101) for _ in range(size)]]
stress.note: narrow value range guarantees many duplicate minimums

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def sumSubarrayMins(self, arr):
        MOD = 10**9 + 7
        n = len(arr)
        # left[i]: distance to previous strictly smaller element (or left edge)
        # right[i]: distance to next smaller-or-equal element (or right edge)
        left = [0] * n
        right = [0] * n
        stack = []
        for i in range(n):
            while stack and arr[stack[-1]] >= arr[i]:
                stack.pop()
            left[i] = i + 1 if not stack else i - stack[-1]
            stack.append(i)
        stack = []
        for i in range(n - 1, -1, -1):
            while stack and arr[stack[-1]] > arr[i]:
                stack.pop()
            right[i] = n - i if not stack else stack[-1] - i
            stack.append(i)
        return sum(arr[i] * left[i] * right[i] for i in range(n)) % MOD
